Description: Datetime representing the birth date and time of the guest.
Foreign key referencing Apartment_Buildings, linking the apartment to its parent building.
First name of the guest.
Primary key uniquely identifying each building in the database.
Datetime representing the end date and time of the apartment reservation.
Character field storing the total number of rooms in the apartment unit.
Character code indicating the current status of the booking, such as Provisional or Confirmed.
Physical street address and location details for the building.
Contact telephone number for the building management.
Datetime representing the start date and time of the apartment reservation.
Primary key uniquely identifying each guest record.
Short name or alias for the building, typically a concise identifier.
Last name of the guest.
Primary key uniquely identifying each apartment booking record.
Character field storing the gender classification of the guest, such as Female or Unknown.
Bit field indicating availability status, where 1 represents available and 0 represents unavailable.

Name of the individual responsible for managing the building.
Part of the primary key and foreign key referencing Apartments, identifying the specific apartment unit.
Identifier for the specific unit within the building, such as Suite number or Apt number.
Integer count of bathrooms contained within the apartment unit.
Foreign key referencing Apartments, identifying the apartment unit for which status is recorded.
Primary key representing the timestamp when the unit status was recorded or became effective.
Part of the primary key representing a facility or amenity available in the apartment, such as Boardband or Swimming Pool.
Text description of the building or its primary unit type, such as Flat or Studio.
Code indicating the type of apartment unit, such as Flat, Duplex, or Studio.

Full official name of the building property.
